title: Birthday Party Plan
goal: Plan a fun backyard birthday party before guests arrive on Saturday.

risks.title: Rain might move the party inside
risks.description: Plan an indoor game layout before the forecast changes.

response.label: Indoor plan ready
response.outcome: The backup layout made the rain forecast much less stressful.

risks.title: Snacks might not fit every guest
risks.description: Check allergies and favorite snacks before shopping.

response.label: Snack check used
response.outcome: The snack list helped avoid last-minute food problems.

risks.title: A helper might be late
risks.description: Share the setup plan so another teammate can cover.

response.label: Setup plan shared
response.outcome: The team covered the late helper without panic.

events.title: Rain is in the forecast
events.body: The party is planned for the backyard, but rain may arrive during game time.

choices.label: Move games under cover
choices.outcome: The party stays fun, but the team uses time rearranging the space.
choices.lesson: Risk responses work best when they protect the main goal.

choices.label: Hope the rain misses
choices.outcome: Nothing changes now, but guests may be uncomfortable later.
choices.lesson: Hope is not a plan when people are depending on you.

choices.label: Cancel the games
choices.outcome: The schedule gets simpler, but the party feels less exciting.
choices.lesson: Cut scope carefully so the project still meets its goal.

events.title: A guest cannot eat the planned snacks
events.body: One guest has a food allergy. The snack table needs a safer option.

choices.label: Buy one safe snack option
choices.outcome: The guest feels included and the budget takes a small hit.
choices.lesson: Quality includes making the result work for real users.

choices.label: Label the snacks clearly
choices.outcome: Guests can choose safely, but the snack table is still limited.
choices.lesson: Clear communication reduces risk even when you cannot fix everything.

choices.label: Ignore it because shopping is done
choices.outcome: The plan stays cheap, but trust drops.
choices.lesson: Ignoring stakeholder needs can make a project feel unsuccessful.

events.title: A helper is running late
events.body: The games need setup, but one helper cannot arrive until later.

choices.label: Reassign the must-have setup
choices.outcome: The team protects the important work and leaves extras for later.

choices.label: Ask everyone to rush
choices.outcome: More work gets done, but the team feels pressured.
choices.lesson: Speed can create hidden quality and morale costs.

choices.label: Skip setup checks
choices.outcome: The party starts sooner, but a game may not work well.
choices.lesson: Skipping validation can move problems to the customer.

events.title: Two extra guests RSVP
events.body: The guest count changed after snacks and games were planned.

choices.label: Check budget and adjust snacks
choices.outcome: The plan changes in a controlled way.
choices.lesson: Scope changes need budget and capacity checks.

choices.label: Add everything immediately
choices.outcome: The extra guests are covered, but the team loses focus.
choices.lesson: Saying yes without tradeoffs can overload the plan.

choices.label: Do nothing
choices.outcome: The team saves money, but the party may feel short on supplies.
choices.lesson: A changed requirement still needs a decision.
